stories.title: Schwarber's Quest — Going for 6 Straight with His Manager's MLB Record in Sight

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Kyle Schwarber tied the Phillies franchise record with 5 straight HR games. Now he goes for 6 tonight at Fenway.

The MLB record of 8 straight belongs to Don Mattingly. Mattingly is currently Schwarber's manager.

Baseball writes its own stories. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Schwarber has 17 HRs. He's tied for the Phillies record with Schmidt, Allen, Abreu, Utley, Hoskins, Herrera, and Turner.

If he hits No. 6 tonight: sole possession of the franchise record.

Phillies at Red Sox. First pitch tonight. ⚾ #Phillies #MLB

article.headline: Schwarber Goes for Six: The HR Streak, the Franchise Record, and His Manager's MLB Mark

research.facts:
Schwarber HR Streak — Going for Six Straight
**Sources:** MLB.com official news, Philadelphia Baseball Review, Washington Post, SI.com Phillies, Yahoo Sports
- Kyle Schwarber hit a first-inning HR at Fenway Park on May 12, going deep for 5th straight game, tying Phillies franchise record (HIGH — MLB.com official)
- Phillies won 2-1 over Red Sox on May 12 (HIGH — multiple sources)
- Schwarber leads MLB with 17 home runs (HIGH — May 12 coverage confirmed)
- 6 HRs in 5-game span, personal best (HIGH — Washington Post)
- Phillies franchise record: 5 consecutive HR games, shared with Dick Allen (1969), Mike Schmidt (1979), Bobby Abreu (2005), Chase Utley (twice in 2008), Rhys Hoskins (2017), Odúbel Herrera (2018), Trea Turner (2023) (HIGH — MLB.com official)
- MLB record: 8 consecutive HR games — Ken Griffey Jr. (Mariners, 1993), Don Mattingly (Yankees, 1987), Dale Long (Pirates, 1956) (HIGH — MLB.com, widely confirmed)
- Don Mattingly is currently the Phillies' interim manager (HIGH — multiple sources from May 13)
- Phillies 20-22, 11-3 under Mattingly (HIGH — May 13 story history)
- Schwarber going for 6th straight tonight at Fenway (HIGH — implied from schedule)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Rico Garcia — The Expansion Era's Most Untouchable Reliever

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Rico Garcia has faced 64 batters this season. He's allowed 1 hit.

That hit was a home run. Every ball in play otherwise: an out. BABIP of .000.

He's the first pitcher in the Expansion Era (since 1961) to do this. ERA: 0.47. ⚾ #Orioles #MLB

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Rico Garcia was a 30th-round pick. He had Tommy John surgery. He was a free agent 7 times. Claimed off waivers 4 times (3 times last year alone).

In 2026, he's the most untouchable reliever in baseball.

Batters are hitting .018 against him. ⚾ #Orioles #MLB

article.headline: One Hit in 64 Batters: Rico Garcia Is the Most Untouchable Reliever in Baseball

image_concepts.label: Image
image_concepts.raw:
Rico Garcia

#### Image Concept 3 — Social (1080x1350)

- **Usage:** X post 10:30 AM + Facebook long-form
- **Alt text:** Rico Garcia pitching in Baltimore Orioles uniform

#### Image Concept 4 — Social (1080x1350)
- **Type:** imagn

research.facts:
Rico Garcia — Expansion Era Reliever Record
**Sources:** MLB.com official news, Yahoo Sports (CA), OriolesHangout.com, Wikipedia
- Rico Garcia is Baltimore Orioles reliever (HIGH)
- First pitcher in Expansion Era (since 1961) to allow at most one hit over first 64 batters faced to start a season (HIGH — MLB.com official)
- First pitcher in MLB history to allow at most one total hit in first 20 appearances in a season (HIGH — MLB.com official)
- Only hit allowed was a home run by Royals' Michael Massey on April 21 (HIGH — MLB.com)
- His BABIP (batting average on balls in play) is .000 (HIGH — all balls in play are outs)
- ERA: 0.47, WHIP: 0.42 (HIGH — MLB.com Statcast profile)
- 22 strikeouts, 7 walks (HIGH — MLB.com)
- Batters hitting .018 against him (HIGH — MLB.com official)
- Journey: 30th-round pick by Rockies in 2016 (HIGH — Wikipedia/multiple sources)
- Tommy John surgery in 2021 (HIGH — BaltimoreBaseball.com article)
- Free agent 7 times (HIGH — Yahoo Sports)
- Claimed off waivers 4 times, including 3 times in 2025 alone (HIGH — Yahoo Sports)
**Confidence:** All facts HIGH.

stories.title: Ben Rice Leads MLB in OPS — The Yankee No One Saw Coming

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
Everyone's watching Judge vs. Murakami in the HR race.

Meanwhile, Yankees 1B Ben Rice leads ALL of MLB with a 1.214 OPS. 13 home runs. Hard-hit rate: 59.6%.

The Bronx has a third dangerous bat nobody is talking about. ⚾ #Yankees #MLB

stories.title: Tampa Bay's Unstoppable May — 16 Wins in 18 Games

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Tampa Bay has won 16 of their last 18 games.

Their ace is out for the season. Two more starters are on the IL. Kevin Cash is using openers.

The Rays are leading the AL East anyway. Don't sleep on Tampa Bay. ⚾ #Rays #MLB

stories.title: Braves Continue to Punish Cubs in Atlanta — Building Biggest NL East Lead

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Atlanta has beaten the Cubs twice in Atlanta this week. Game 3 is tonight.

The Braves lead the NL in fewest runs allowed (3.44/game). The Cubs had two 10-game win streaks in April.

This series is a preview of the NL playoff picture. ⚾ #Braves #Cubs #MLB

article.headline: The Braves Have Beaten the Cubs Twice. Game 3 Today Will Tell Us Who the NL's Best Team Actually Is.

research.facts:
Braves-Cubs Series
**Sources:** ESPN game pages, MLB.com standings, story history from May 13
- Braves beat Cubs 5-2 in Game 1 on May 12 (combined 1-hitter; Grant Holmes + relievers) (HIGH — story history)
- Braves beat Cubs 4-1 in Game 2 on May 13 (HIGH — May 13 MLB scores from web search)
- Braves record approximately 30-13 after May 13 win (MEDIUM — exact record confirmed at 29-13 May 12, Win on May 13 = 30-13; verify)
- Cubs record 27-15 after May 13 loss (HIGH — from pipeline status, loses one = 27-16)
- Braves lead MLB in fewest runs allowed per game at 3.44 (HIGH — May 13 story analysis)
- Cubs had two 10-game win streaks in first 40 decisions — first since 1955 Dodgers (HIGH — story history)
- Game 3 scheduled May 14 at Truist Park (HIGH — series schedule)
**Confidence:** Braves 30-13 record is MEDIUM (calculated from 29-13 + win; use as "approximately"). All other facts HIGH.
